text.tspan: A,B two points on an horizontal axis
lAB: distance between A and B
a: angle of the tangent at A
b: angle of the tangent at B
c: angle of the tangent at C
Notice that the position of C is not defined yet
Find an arc-of-circle AC respecting the tangents in A and C
and an arc-of-circle CB respecting the tangents in C and B?
Where should be located C to enable those 2 arc-of-circles?
The arc AC has its center in E because of the tangents.
The triangle AEC must also be isoscel in E
Find u=aBAC, so AEC is isocel.
aDEA = Pi - aEAD - aADE = a+c = aCEA
The arc CB has its center in F because of the tangents.
The triangle BFC must also be isoscel in F
Find v=aABC, so BFC is isocel.
aBFD = Pi - aDBF - aFDB = b-c = aBFC
